title: paspalum scrobiculatum var. thunbergii
intro: Paspalum scrobiculatum var. thunbergii, also known as Japanese millet or Japanese barnyard grass, is a type of grass species that is native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Paspalum scrobiculatum var. thunbergii include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce oxidative stress, and improve cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of this plant include dried roots and leaves, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and extracts that can be used to treat various health conditions.
